front: What is the formal definition of a function from set A to set B?
back: A function f from a set A to a set B is a relation from A to B such that for every element a ∈ A, there exists exactly one element b ∈ B with (a, b) ∈ f. This is denoted as f: A → B.

A function f: A → B is one-to-one (or injective) if and only if distinct elements in the domain A map to distinct elements in the codomain B. Formally: if f(a₁) = f(a₂), then a₁ = a₂ for all a₁, a₂ ∈ A.

front: What is the key difference between a plain function and a one-to-one function?
back: A plain function allows multiple inputs to map to the same output. A one-to-one function does not; each input has a unique output. All one-to-one functions are functions, but not all functions are one-to-one.

front: What is the Horizontal Line Test used for?
back: The Horizontal Line Test is a graphical method to determine if a function is one-to-one. If no horizontal line intersects the graph of the function more than once, then the function is injective.

front: What is the formal definition of the identity function? Provide an example.
back: The identity function on a set A, denoted idₐ or I, is a function from A to A defined by idₐ(a) = a for every a ∈ A. It is the simplest example of a one-to-one function. For example, for A = {1, 2, 3}, idₐ = {(1,1), (2,2), (3,3)}.
